_meta.purpose: Fundamental conviction layer for TAO Monitor. Qualitative reads authored by Claude/Simon, surfaced (NOT auto-generated) by the cron. Slow-moving — updated on review, not every cycle.
_meta.note: Each read is stamped with 'reviewed'. The cron flags a read older than staleness_days, and pings on quant-vs-verdict divergence (e.g. AVOID name with rising net TAO inflow = promo pump; KEEP name with falling usage = re-review).

9.name: IOTA
9.team: Macrocosmos — established multi-subnet team; arXiv primer, Train-at-Home app
9.traction: High — real training, active miners, credible research
9.why: Top-tier team and execution. Open question: who ultimately pays for the models (research infra, pre-revenue).

4.name: Targon
4.what: Confidential GPU compute for regulated industries (TVM)
4.team: Manifold Labs — Rob Myers (ex-OTF core), James Woodman (ex-OTF COO)
4.why: Real team, real scale, genuine commercial angle. Upside capped by size (already large).

44.name: Score
44.team: Score Technologies (Max Sebti); live product scorevision.io
44.traction: Medium — live product, Reading FC partner; adoption proof still limited
44.why: Rare real external customer (Reading FC); 19MB model beat frontier on a detection benchmark. Your -15% is entry-price, not quality.

68.name: NOVA
68.team: Metanova Labs — CEO Micaela Bazo, crypto-native biotech
68.why: Huge TAM, credible team, real activity — but most speculative on outcomes (drug discovery is long, ~90% fail). Size small.

110.name: Green Compute
110.team: Josh Riddett (Easy Crypto Hunter, UK GPU-mining); lead investors DSV — Siam Kidd, Mark Creaser
110.why: Promoter-backed (DSV/Siam), pre-traction; stock-photo testimonials and implausible certs. Revisit only on real usage + published buyback tx hashes.
